title: RSS-feed discovery fetches source URLs through raw requests bypassing robots.txt/SSRF guard/per-host rate limit
evidence: discover_rss_feeds() and _validate_rss_feed() called requests.get/head directly (lines 303,346,390) with no robots check and no _guard_target SSRF check; only a 2s GLOBAL delay (MIN_DELAY_SECONDS), not per-host. The EthicalFetcher's robots-fail-closed + SSRF guard were not on this path.
reproduction: POST /api/sources/discover/rss with a source whose URL is disallowed by robots.txt or points at an internal address; the fetch proceeded.
recommended_fix: Route discovery fetches through EthicalFetcher (require_html=False) so robots/SSRF/rate-limit apply uniformly.
risk_if_unfixed: A second fetch path silently bypasses the ethical-scraping invariant that must not be bypassable

title: Topic discovery posts queries to DuckDuckGo (external service) - a cloud call reachable from the app
evidence: DuckDuckGoSearch.search() POSTs to BASE_URL (DuckDuckGo HTML endpoint) at line 150; reached via POST /api/sources/discover/topic. User-triggered, not in the default ingestion path, but it is an outbound third-party call from a local-first tool.
reproduction: POST /api/sources/discover/topic with topic=x -> outbound HTTPS POST to duckduckgo.com
recommended_fix: Document the call as an opt-in external lookup; gate behind an explicit setting; ensure it is never invoked by the default/scheduled path.
risk_if_unfixed: Operator may not realize a query leaves the machine; erodes local-first trust if ever wired into an automated path

title: Dangerous defaults in .env.example contradict loopback-only posture
evidence: .env.example:56 UVICORN_HOST=0.0.0.0, :61 OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0, :62 OLLAMA_ORIGINS=*. Following the template would bind services to all interfaces / allow any origin to the local LLM. The running app actually binds 127.0.0.1 via OO_HOST (main.py:1108).
recommended_fix: Set safe defaults (loopback binds, scoped LLM origins) with inline comments; rewrote .env.example to the real surface.
risk_if_unfixed: Operator copying the template exposes the LLM daemon and app to the network

title: env/Config ship auth/JWT secrets (SECRET_KEY/CSRF_SECRET/JWT_*) for an auth system that does not exist
evidence: settings.py read SECRET_KEY/CSRF_SECRET into Config but no code used them (no auth routes; CSRF is an Origin/Referer loopback check in main.py:300).
reproduction: grep -rn 'secret_key|csrf_secret' src/ showed only the settings.py read, no use
recommended_fix: Removed the unused auth/JWT/secret fields from .env.example and Config.
risk_if_unfixed: Operators generate and protect secrets that do nothing; false sense of an auth layer

title: Cache key uses SHA1 without usedforsecurity=False (bandit B324)
evidence: bandit flagged hashlib.sha1 at cache.py:336. It hashes a repr() for an in-memory cache key - not a security boundary - but tripped SAST.
recommended_fix: Pass usedforsecurity=False; bandit on src/ now reports 0 issues.
risk_if_unfixed: SAST noise; no real exposure

title: Weak MD5 hashing in dead 'ingestor' package (bandit B324)
evidence: bandit flagged hashlib.md5 in the ingestor package, which was imported by no live code; no live exposure.
recommended_fix: Resolved by MAINT-01: the ingestor package moved to quarantine/dead_src (excluded from src/ SAST scope).
risk_if_unfixed: None while dead; would matter only if revived

title: Vestigial ollama_host='0.0.0.0' default in Config (bandit B104)
evidence: settings.py ollama_host default was '0.0.0.0' (bind-all). The fields had no live consumers but mirrored the SEC-01 dangerous posture.
recommended_fix: Defaulted ollama_host/origins to loopback and auto_download_models to False.
risk_if_unfixed: Misleading default; no live exposure

title: Tests validated the legacy ingestor.url_utils via sys.path hack instead of live src/utils/url_utils
evidence: test_url_utils.py and test_scraper.py used sys.path.append('.../src') then imported from ingestor.url_utils. That module was a re-export shim of src.utils.url_utils (coverage was indirect but real), still coupling the test to a dead package.
recommended_fix: Repointed test_url_utils.py at src.utils.url_utils, dropped the sys.path hack; test_scraper.py handled by MAINT-01.
risk_if_unfixed: Dedup/canonicalization regressions could pass CI via the wrong module

title: No retry/backoff on transient fetch failures despite config/docs promising it
evidence: EthicalFetcher.fetch does a single attempt; Config exposes max_retries/initial_retry_delay and tenacity is a core dep, but the live fetch path ignores them.
recommended_fix: Phase 4 (reliability): implement bounded retry/backoff on idempotent GETs honoring max_retries, or remove the unused config.
risk_if_unfixed: Transient blips drop articles silently; config knob is a no-op

title: App version reported three different ways (0.02 / 0.03 / 0.0.7)
evidence: settings.py app_version default '0.02'; settings.yaml version '0.03'; pyproject/README/health '0.0.7'.
reproduction: grep -n version configs/settings.yaml; grep -n app_version src/config/settings.py
recommended_fix: Single-sourced app_version from importlib.metadata (_package_version); unmapped the YAML version key. Config().app_version == 0.0.7 verified.
risk_if_unfixed: Version shown in UI/logs may be wrong; erodes provenance/trust

title: close_session swallowed all exceptions silently
evidence: session.py close_session used 'except Exception: pass' - hides connection-leak symptoms.
recommended_fix: Log at debug (exc_info) before swallowing; cleanup still never raises.
risk_if_unfixed: Masks resource-cleanup failures during debugging

title: Broad except Exception around HTML parsing/feed validation hides discovery failures
evidence: duckduckgo.py outer handlers catch bare Exception; can fail or partially succeed silently.
recommended_fix: Phase 4: narrow to parser/fetch exceptions; the ETH-01 rework already replaced the inner fetch try/except with FetchError handling.
risk_if_unfixed: Discovery silently returns empty/partial results

title: Verify near-duplicate LSH banding bounds pairwise comparisons (claimed near-linear)
evidence: near_dup.py implements MinHash + LSH banding + union-find - the right design - but has no benchmark proving it scales.
recommended_fix: Phase 4 benchmark: signature+LSH time vs corpus size (1k/10k/50k); tune bands/rows.
risk_if_unfixed: Could degrade to O(n^2) on large corpora if banding mis-tuned; unverified

title: Article list/search query latency at scale unverified
evidence: dedup stage-1 _exists(canonical_url=...) is a point lookup (indexed). But /api/articles search + list need EXPLAIN on a seeded DB to confirm index usage and pagination cost.
recommended_fix: Phase 4: seed 50k rows, EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN on /api/articles filters + FTS; add missing indexes.
risk_if_unfixed: Unverified query latency at scale

title: No test for max body-size cap rejection
evidence: Body-size enforcement existed (__init__.py:260-273) but no test exercised an over-cap response.
recommended_fix: Added tests/test_fetcher_limits.py: declared Content-Length and materialised body over cap both rejected.
risk_if_unfixed: A regression removing the cap would not be caught; OOM risk on large pages

title: No test for redirect-cap termination
evidence: _MAX_REDIRECTS=5 with per-hop re-validation was untested for the over-limit case.
recommended_fix: Added test_fetcher_limits.py: a redirect chain terminates at the cap (FetchFailed 'too many redirects').
risk_if_unfixed: Redirect-loop / SSRF-via-redirect regressions uncaught

title: No test for SSRF guard resolving a hostname to an internal address (DNS rebinding)
evidence: _guard_target resolves and checks IPs but had no test; the guard also re-resolves at connect time so a TOCTOU window remains.
recommended_fix: Added test_fetcher_limits.py: hostnames resolving to 127.0.0.1 / 169.254.169.254 are refused. Residual TOCTOU (connect-time IP pinning) deferred to Phase 4 hardening.
risk_if_unfixed: Exotic but real SSRF bypass via DNS rebinding

title: Rate-limit sleep duration not asserted
evidence: Rate-limit tests set min_interval_s=0; no test asserts the actual delay is honored.
recommended_fix: Phase 4: add a fake-clock test asserting the sleep equals max(min_interval, crawl_delay).
risk_if_unfixed: Politeness-delay regression uncaught

title: Several API modules lack endpoint tests (keyword_management, reporting, framing; llm only model-level)
evidence: Endpoint-coverage pass found no dedicated test files for these routers; test_route_smoke covers parameterless GETs only.
recommended_fix: Phase 4: add endpoint smoke/integration tests for the uncovered POST/PUT/DELETE handlers.
risk_if_unfixed: POST/PUT/DELETE handlers can break undetected

title: Suite was RED on a core-only install (analysis-extra tests failed instead of skipping)
evidence: With only [dev] installed: 4 collection errors (scipy) + 11 endpoint failures (analysis routers 404). Green only with [analysis].
reproduction: pip install -e '.[dev]' in a clean venv; pytest tests/ -> 4 errors + 11 failures
recommended_fix: conftest.py detects the extra and collect_ignores the 8 analysis-dependent modules when absent. Verified: core venv 717 passed / 0 failed.
risk_if_unfixed: 'extras are optional' contract is false; contributors on core install see a red suite

title: ~4360 LOC of dead packages inside src/ (ingestor, scraper, custom_types, compliance, audit, reports)
evidence: AST import graph (Phase 1): six packages imported by no live code; only tie was a side-effect import in src/__init__.py. Confirmed src/scraper dead too (only consumer was dead ingestor/pipeline).
reproduction: import_graph.py isolated list; grep for live importers (none)
recommended_fix: Moved all six packages + their test_scraper.py to quarantine/dead_src; trimmed src/__init__.py to import only crypto. Suite green; app boots with all routes.
risk_if_unfixed: Dead code rots, confuses contributors, hides real coverage, carries latent issues

title: 1055 ruff lint errors and 259 files needing format
evidence: ruff check . -> 1055 errors (284 E402, 234 F401, 150 UP006, 107 UP035, 33 F841, 7 E722, ...); ruff format --check -> 259 files. CI runs ruff continue-on-error.
recommended_fix: Phase 4: ruff --fix + format as a separate style: commit; then flip CI ruff to blocking. (Dead-code removal already cut some.)
risk_if_unfixed: Style debt obscures real diffs; advisory CI lets it grow

title: 317 mypy errors across 55 files
evidence: mypy src/ -> 317 errors in 55 files (e.g. main.py:803 Column[int] vs int at an API boundary).
recommended_fix: Phase 4: fix high-signal errors on core paths first; wire mypy into CI advisory->blocking.
risk_if_unfixed: Type errors mask real bugs

title: print() calls in src/ instead of the configured logger
evidence: 91 print() in src/, concentrated in utils/cache.py (17), services/duckduckgo.py (11), crypto/provenance.py (10); 22 were in the now-quarantined dead packages.
recommended_fix: Phase 4: replace remaining live print() with module loggers (structlog is a core dep). MAINT-01 removed the dead-package prints.
risk_if_unfixed: Unstructured stdout noise; bypasses log levels/audit

title: .env.example omitted ~21 real OO_* env vars and documented ~10 unused ones
evidence: .env.example omitted OO_DATA_DIR/OO_HOST/OO_PORT/OO_FETCH_*/OO_NO_*/OO_OLLAMA_URL/OO_LLM_MODEL/DB_POOL_SIZE/... and documented UVICORN_*/POSTGRES_*/JWT_* that code never reads.
recommended_fix: Rewrote .env.example to the real OO_* surface, safe-by-default, with the unused vars removed.
risk_if_unfixed: Operators cannot discover real config; documents fiction

title: configs/settings.yaml is half-dead (~13 documented keys never read)
evidence: Only ~12 of ~25 keys are read by _apply_yaml_config; frontend.*, export.*, audit.scrape_log/error_log, logging.max_log_size/backup_count, security.cors_allow_methods/headers are ignored.
recommended_fix: Phase 5: prune unread keys or wire them; reconcile with the config-driven-design invariant.
risk_if_unfixed: Config that looks authoritative but is inert

title: ~69MB of prior-audit JSON committed (single file 62.9MB)
evidence: docs/archive: QUBS_PHASE2_FULL_REPORT.json 62.9MB, PHASE2 3.1MB, PHASE3 1.8MB. Inflates every clone of an offline/Qubes-targeted repo.
recommended_fix: Phase 5: keep findings indexes; move/compress the large dumps; git history retains them.
risk_if_unfixed: Clone/storage bloat for a local-first tool

title: CI push-trigger excluded the default branch (0.07)
evidence: ci.yml push branches were ['0.04','claude/**'] while remote HEAD is 0.07. Direct pushes to 0.05/0.06/0.07 ran no push-CI.
reproduction: grep -n branches .github/workflows/ci.yml; git remote show origin | grep HEAD
recommended_fix: Changed push trigger to branches-ignore: [] (every pushed branch), immune to release-cycle renames.
risk_if_unfixed: Unchecked code lands on the default branch via direct push

title: Postgres path is untested scaffolding; search is SQLite-FTS5 only
evidence: session.py branches for Postgres pooling but ensure_fts no-ops on non-SQLite; suite never runs against Postgres; WAL/busy-timeout pragmas are SQLite-only. Docs imply dual support.
recommended_fix: Phase 4/5: commit to SQLite-only in docs (honest), or add a Postgres FTS path + CI matrix.
risk_if_unfixed: Operators choosing Postgres get a search-less, untested deployment
